evidence.text: We expect the new ISG engine, which began production in the second quarter of 2014 with our Beijing Foton Cummins Engine Co., Ltd. joint venture, to continue to gain market share in China in its first full year of production.

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation has shown mixed performance. While the Cash Flows subsection indicates a decrease in free cash flow, the Other Operating (Expense) Income, Net subsection suggests stability with no significant changes.

evidence.text: Net cash provided by operating activities decreased for the nine months ended September 27, 2015, primarily due to unfavorable working capital fluctuations, partially offset by higher consolidated net income.

Other operating (expense) income, net was as follows: Three months ended September 27, 2015, compared to the comparable period in 2014, showed a decrease in amortization of intangible assets and other expenses.

factors.summary: The company has implemented restructuring and cost reduction programs, including workforce reductions of up to 2,000 employees and potential manufacturing facility closures. These actions are expected to incur significant pre-tax charges ranging from $70 million to $90 million in the fourth quarter, with possible additional charges from facility closures. While the cost reductions aim to improve efficiency, the associated charges and workforce reductions indicate challenges in managing expenses.

evidence.text: Restructuring actions were announced to reduce employee workforce by up to 2,000 employees, incurring a pre-tax charge of $70 million to $90 million.
The company will incur a pre-tax charge in the fourth quarter.

Overall, selling, general and administrative expenses, as a percentage of sales, increased to 11.5 percent in the three months ended September 27, 2015, from 10.8 percent in the comparable period in 2014.

We announced we will reduce our worldwide professional work force by up to 2,000 employees in response to lower demand for our products in the U.S. and key markets around the world. The employee reductions will come from all parts of the company. We will incur a pre-tax fourth quarter charge in the range of $70 million to $90 million for the headcount reductions. [...] We expect to close or restructure several manufacturing facilities over time which could increase the fourth quarter charge and may result in additional charges in the future.

factors.summary: The margin trajectory is mixed, with some improvement in gross margins but downward pressure on operating margins due to factors like foreign currency fluctuations, lower volumes, and pricing pressures. While there are signs of cost management and supply chain effectiveness, these are partially offset by challenges in certain segments and overall market conditions.

evidence.text: Gross margin increased for the nine months ended September 27, 2015, versus the comparable period in 2014, and increased as a percentage of sales by 0.6 percentage points.

Distribution segment EBIT for the three months ended September 27, 2015, decreased versus the comparable period in 2014, primarily due to unfavorable foreign currency fluctuations (primarily in Australia and Canada), partially offset by the acquisition of North American distributors.

The decrease in net income and earnings per diluted share was driven by lower gross margin, unfavorable foreign currency fluctuations, and lower equity, royalty and interest income from investees, partially offset by a lower effective tax rate.
Solid balance sheet and credit ratings enable ready access to credit and capital markets.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is decelerating across multiple segments and international markets, primarily due to lower demand, unfavorable economic conditions, and foreign currency fluctuations. While some areas saw slight improvements, the overall trend indicates a negative direction.

evidence.text: Components segment sales for the three months ended September 27, 2015, decreased versus the comparable period in 2014.

The following were the primary drivers by business: •Foreign currency fluctuations unfavorably impacted sales results (primarily in Europe, Brazil and Australia). •Turbo technologies sales decreased primarily due to lower demand in China, Europe and Latin America. •Filtration sales decreased primarily due to lower demand in the North American on-highway markets and lower demand in Europe, North America and Latin America.
Net sales for the three months ended September 27, 2015, decreased versus the comparable period in 2014.

Worldwide revenues decreased 6 percent in the three months ended September 27, 2015, as compared to the same period in 2014 [...] partially offset by sales increases related to the consolidation of partially-owned North American distributors since December 31, 2013. Continued international economic weakness in the third quarter of 2015 negatively impacted our international revenues [...] which declined by 18 percent, with sales down in most of our markets, primarily in Brazil, as a result of challenging economic conditions and slower growth in China.

factors.summary: Demand trends are mixed across different segments and regions, with growth observed in certain areas like North America and China, while declines are seen in others such as Europe and Latin America. Overall, there is a noticeable deceleration in demand, particularly in international markets, driven by factors like lower global industrial demand and economic weaknesses in regions like Brazil and China.

evidence.text: Components segment sales for the three months ended September 27, 2015, decreased versus the comparable period in 2014. The following were the primary drivers by business: •Foreign currency fluctuations unfavorably impacted sales results (primarily in Europe, Brazil and Australia). •Turbo technologies sales decreased primarily due to lower demand in China, Europe and Latin America. •Filtration sales decreased primarily due to lower demand in the North American on-highway markets and lower demand in Europe, North America and Latin America. The decreases above were partially offset by improved emission solutions sales, primarily due to improved demand in certain North American on-highway markets and higher demand in China, partially offset by lower demand in Brazil.

The primary drivers by segment were as follows: •Engine segment sales decrease d by 10 percent primarily due to lower demand in most global industrial markets as well as lower demand in international on-highway markets, partially offset by higher demand in global bus markets and North American medium-duty truck markets.

Worldwide revenues decreased 6 percent in the three months ended September 27, 2015, as compared to the same period in 2014, primarily due to unfavorable foreign currency fluctuations, lower global demand in most industrial markets, lower on-highway demand in international markets and weakness in global power generation markets, partially offset by sales increases related to the consolidation of partially-owned North American distributors since December 31, 2013. Continued international economic weakness in the third quarter of 2015 negatively impacted our international revenues (excluding the United States and Canada), which declined by 18 percent, with sales down in most of our markets, primarily in Brazil, as a result of challenging economic conditions and slower growth in China.

factors.summary: The end market momentum is mixed across different regions and sectors. While North American on-highway markets and China show strength, other regions like Europe, Latin America, and certain sectors such as construction and commercial marine are facing challenges with lower demand.

evidence.text: Components segment sales for the three months ended September 27, 2015, decreased versus the comparable period in 2014. The following were the primary drivers by business: •Foreign currency fluctuations unfavorably impacted sales results (primarily in Europe, Brazil and Australia). •Turbo technologies sales decreased primarily due to lower demand in China, Europe and Latin America. •Filtration sales decreased primarily due to lower demand in the North American on-highway markets and lower demand in Europe, North America and Latin America. The decreases above were partially offset by improved emission solutions sales, primarily due to improved demand in certain North American on-highway markets and higher demand in China, partially offset by lower demand in Brazil.

Construction market (primarily in Europe) and the commercial marine market and lower demand in on-highway markets (primarily in Brazil and China). [...] We expect the challenging conditions described above to persist for some time. [...] We expect demand in the North American medium-duty truck market to remain stable. [...] We expect North American light-duty demand to remain stable.

factors.summary: Input costs are generally stable or declining, particularly in materials and commodities, which have helped offset some unfavorable factors. However, despite lower material and commodity costs, there are still pressures impacting gross margins, such as lower volumes and unfavorable foreign currency fluctuations.

evidence.text: The decrease in gross margin for the three months ended September 27, 2015, versus the comparable period in 2014, was primarily due to lower volumes and unfavorable mix, partially offset by lower material and commodity costs and favorable foreign currency fluctuations.
Lower material and commodity costs were noted, which helped offset some unfavorable factors.

The decrease in gross margin was primarily due to lower volumes, unfavorable foreign currency fluctuations [...] partially offset by improved Distribution segment sales related to the consolidation of partially-owned North American distributors since December 31, 2013, lower material and commodity costs and lower warranty costs.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are declining across most markets, particularly in international regions like China, Europe, Latin America, and Brazil, due to lower demand and economic downturns. The decline is evident in various product lines, including Turbo technologies and Filtration, and is exacerbated by unfavorable foreign currency fluctuations.

evidence.text: Components segment sales for the three months ended September 27, 2015, decreased versus the comparable period in 2014. The following were the primary drivers by business: •Foreign currency fluctuations unfavorably impacted sales results (primarily in Europe, Brazil and Australia). •Turbo technologies sales decreased primarily due to lower demand in China, Europe and Latin America. •Filtration sales decreased primarily due to lower demand in the North American on-highway markets and lower demand in Europe, North America and Latin America. The decreases above were partially offset by improved emission solutions sales, primarily due to improved demand in certain North American on-highway markets and higher demand in China, partially offset by lower demand in Brazil.

Unit shipments by engine classification (including unit shipments to Power Generation) were as follows: Three months ended September 27, 2015, compared to the comparable period in 2014, showed a decrease in mid-range, heavy-duty, and high-horsepower engine shipments.

Worldwide revenues decreased 6 percent in the three months ended September 27, 2015, as compared to the same period in 2014 [...] primarily due to unfavorable foreign currency fluctuations, lower global demand in most industrial markets, lower on-highway demand in international markets and weakness in global power generation markets [...] international revenues (excluding the United States and Canada) declined by 18 percent, with sales down in most of our markets, primarily in Brazil, as a result of challenging economic conditions and slower growth in China.

factors.summary: Mergers and acquisitions (M&A) activity, particularly the consolidation of North American distributors, have had mixed impacts on segment performance. While there have been positive contributions from increased sales due to acquisitions, these have been partially offset by negative factors such as foreign currency fluctuations and decreased sales in certain regions.

evidence.text: Distribution segment sales for the three months ended September 27, 2015, increased versus the comparable period in 2014, primarily due to $357 million of segment sales related to the consolidation of partially-owned North American distributors since December 31, 2013, and $24 million of organic sales growth primarily in Africa, partially offset by unfavorable foreign currency fluctuations (primarily in Australia, Canada, Europe and South Africa) and decreased sales in Western Europe, Asia Pacific, China and Russia.

The increases above were partially offset by the following: •Foreign currency fluctuations unfavorably impacted sales by approximately 4 percent (primarily in Europe, Brazil, Australia, Canada, India and the U.K.).

factors.summary: The company is experiencing challenges in maintaining pricing power, with evidence of margin compression due to unfavorable pricing. Economic downturns and competitive pressures are contributing to price reductions in certain markets, impacting both gross margins and overall pricing strategies.

evidence.text: The decrease in gross margin was primarily due to... unfavorable pricing.

Economic downturns in markets we serve generally result in reduced sales of our products and can result in price reductions in certain products and/or markets. [...] Competitor pricing activity is also noted as a risk factor.

factors.summary: Construction and infrastructure spending is weak, particularly in Europe, which has led to a decline in demand for industrial engines and related products. This weakness is evident across multiple regions, including North America and Europe, with significant decreases in engine shipments.

evidence.text: Industrial engine sales decrease primarily due to lower global demand in construction markets with decreased engine shipments of 31 percent, primarily in Europe and North America.
Lower demand in international industrial markets led by declines in the construction market (primarily in Europe) and the commercial marine market.

These include weak economic conditions in key regions such as Brazil, China, and Europe, foreign currency volatility impacting sales, and reduced demand in oil and gas markets due to low crude oil prices.

factors.summary: The company's long-term strategic focus is centered on innovation, market expansion, and addressing key macroeconomic trends such as tightening emissions controls, infrastructure needs in emerging markets, energy availability and cost issues, and globalization. There is a consistent emphasis on developing new products to meet future emission standards and improving fuel economy performance, which aligns with the broader strategic direction aimed at ensuring long-term profitable growth.

evidence.text: Research activities continue to focus on development of new products to meet future emission standards around the world and improvements in fuel economy performance.

We believe that, over the longer term, there will be economic improvements in most of our current markets and that our opportunities for long-term profitable growth will continue as the result of the following four macroeconomic trends that should benefit our businesses: tightening emissions controls across the world; infrastructure needs in emerging markets; energy availability and cost issues; and globalization of industries like ours.
